overview.title: Overview
overview.body: The passwd-sso browser extension ("the Extension") is a companion to the passwd-sso password manager. This policy explains what data the Extension accesses, how it is used, and your rights.

dataCollection.title: Data Collection
dataCollection.body: The Extension does NOT collect, transmit, or sell any personal data to third parties. All vault data is end-to-end encrypted and only decrypted locally in your browser.

dataAccessed.title: Data Accessed Locally
dataAccessed.body: The Extension accesses the following data solely on your device:

items.storage: Extension settings and encrypted session state (via chrome.storage)
items.activeTab: The URL of the current tab to match saved credentials for autofill
items.clipboard: Temporarily writes passwords or usernames to the clipboard when you explicitly request it
items.forms: Login form fields on web pages to provide autofill functionality

serverCommunication.title: Server Communication
serverCommunication.body: The Extension communicates exclusively with your self-hosted passwd-sso server instance. No data is sent to any other server. All communication uses HTTPS and encrypted payloads.

permissions.title: Browser Permissions
permissions.body: The Extension requests the following permissions:

items.storage: Save extension settings and encrypted session state
items.alarms: Auto-lock the vault after a configurable timeout period
items.activeTab: Detect the current page URL for credential matching and autofill
items.scripting: Inject autofill scripts into web page forms
items.contextMenus: Provide right-click menu options for quick copy and autofill
items.clipboardWrite: Copy passwords and usernames to the clipboard on demand
items.offscreen: Perform clipboard operations in the background
items.hostPermissions: Access web pages for form detection and autofill (granted on demand)

security.title: Security
security.body: All password data is encrypted client-side using AES-256-GCM before transmission. The master passphrase never leaves your device. Key derivation uses PBKDF2 with 600,000 iterations.

changes.title: Changes to This Policy
changes.body: We may update this policy from time to time. Changes will be reflected on this page with an updated date.

contact.title: Contact
contact.body: If you have questions about this privacy policy, please open an issue on the project repository.
